section_4_introduction.note: 2 paragraphs, motivates all four sub-topics (modules, stdlib, pip/venv, Poetry/Pytest)

[2] https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/v.note: resource's own notes field covers both venv AND pip ('creating/activating virtual environments, installing packages'), so citing [2] for the pip/PyPI claims in 5.3 is a correct source match, not a mismatch

standalone_claim_coverage_estimate.method: Enumerated standalone factual assertions per sub-section (5.1-5.6), Implementation (6), and Real-World Patterns (7), excluding code blocks, worked-example arithmetic/illustration sentences, and author-synthesis/framing sentences that restate an already-cited claim (same exclusion methodology used in the 3.1 and 3.2 corpus-critic passes).
standalone_claim_coverage_estimate.5.1_modules_and_imports: 3/6 cited (50%) -- the three import/from-import/as semantic sentences sit uncited between two [1]-cited sentences (module def, namespacing) on the same resource/topic; treated as part of the same cited cluster rather than a fresh unsupported claim, consistent with how 3.1's minor single-sentence gaps were treated as non-blocking rather than hard misses.
standalone_claim_coverage_estimate.5.3_pip: 2/3 cited (67%, excluding the worked pip-install-requests illustration and the import-vs-pip-install distinction framing)
standalone_claim_coverage_estimate.5.4_venv: 2/3 cited (67%, treating the 4-step create/activate/install/deactivate list as one unit closed out by [2])
standalone_claim_coverage_estimate.5.5_poetry: 3/3 cited (100%, excluding the closing concept-framing sentence)
standalone_claim_coverage_estimate.5.6_pytest: 3/4 cited (75%, excluding the closing concept-framing sentence)
standalone_claim_coverage_estimate.6_implementation: 0/6 cited -- procedural how-to steps restating already-cited 5.1-5.6 concepts (poetry new, venv activate, pip install, import forms, pytest); same treatment as 3.2's uncited Implementation section
standalone_claim_coverage_estimate.aggregate_excl_implementation: 21/28 standalone claims cited (~75%), comfortably above the 60% strict-mode floor
isolated_uncited_gaps_flagged_non_blocki: 5.1: the three sentences explaining what each of import / from...import / import...as actually does are individually uncited, though bracketed by [1]-cited sentences on the same topic from the same resource
5.2: closing 'point of the standard library...hundreds of modules' sentence
5.3: opening 'stdlib is large but cannot cover everything' sentence
5.4: opening 'installing globally is a problem' sentence and closing 'every serious project gets its own venv' framing

prior_concepts_checked.1.x_2.x: concepts_introduced arrays for 1.1-2.2 are empty in sequence.index.json (not yet corpus-drafted); baseline evident subject matter (variables, for/while loops, default arguments, .py files/interpreter) treated as fair-game vocabulary per module prerequisites (1.1, 2.2 listed as this topic's own prerequisites) and consistent with prior critic passes' treatment.

full_corpus_scan.method: Read the full topic_corpus.md and cross-checked every technical noun phrase and every code sample against the concept lists above.
full_corpus_scan.in_scope_confirmed: import / from...import / import...as, namespacing, packages, random/math/datetime, pip, PyPI, venv (create/activate/install/deactivate), Poetry (poetry new/add/install/run, pyproject.toml), Pytest (test_ discovery, assert) -- all map directly onto 3.3's own concepts_introduced.
full_corpus_scan.backward_references_confirmed_ok: Introduction's mention of `def`, loops, `*args`, decorators is explicitly backward-looking ('every tool you have used to write it... came built into Python itself'), referencing 3.1 (*args) and 3.2 (decorators) and 2.x (loops) -- not new forward material.
full_corpus_scan.watch_item_not_flagged_as_a_failure: 5.3 names the runtime error `ModuleNotFoundError` when describing what happens if a package is not installed ('fails with a ModuleNotFoundError before installation'). Formal exception handling (try/except, 'Errors & Exceptions') is topic 7.2, far later in the sequence. However, this is a purely descriptive naming of a runtime error message -- no try/except syntax is used or required to follow the sentence, and no exception-handling behavior is taught or exercised. Judged non-blocking, consistent with how 3.2's parenthetical 'list comprehension (a later topic)' mention was judged acceptable pedagogical/descriptive language rather than a syntax-level forward reference -- this is a slightly higher-risk case since it is not explicitly flagged as 'a later topic,' but it does not rise to the level of requiring or demonstrating unstudied syntax.
other_candidates_checked_and_cleared: round() builtin in the Pytest code example -- baseline builtin-function vocabulary, not a taught 'concept' of this or any listed topic, consistent with prior passes' treatment of baseline builtins (e.g. 3.2's time.time()).
default argument `roll(sides=6)` in the Hands-On Exercise -- explicitly 3.1's own concept (positional/keyword/default arguments), not a forward reference.

f-strings, list/dict comprehensions, classes, exceptions-as-syntax, iterator protocol -- none appear anywhere in the corpus (grep-verified for comprehension/fixture/unittest/TestCase/conftest/parametrize/@pytest patterns, all absent).

poetry_pytest_concept_level_verification.method: Independently re-read sections 5.4 (venv), 5.5 (Poetry), 5.6 (Pytest), 6 (Implementation), and 9 (Hands-On Exercise) in full, plus grepped the whole corpus for fixture|unittest|TestCase|conftest|parametrize|@pytest|self\.assert|live terminal patterns, rather than trusting the corpus-builder's self-report.
poetry_pytest_concept_level_verification.venv_no_live_terminal_assumption: Confirmed. Section 5.4 explicitly frames the create/activate/install/deactivate workflow as 'described here rather than run live, since it depends on a real terminal.' The Hands-On Exercise (section 9) explicitly asks the learner to 'sketch (on paper, or as a comment block -- no live terminal required) the four venv commands' rather than execute them.
poetry_pytest_concept_level_verification.poetry_concept_only: Confirmed. Section 5.5 and Implementation step 1/3 describe poetry new / poetry add / poetry install / poetry run narratively (what each command scaffolds/does and why), with no instruction to actually run a live Poetry command, no assumption of a live terminal session, and an explicit closing line ('At the concept level this topic asks you to hold onto...') anchoring it as conceptual.
poetry_pytest_concept_level_verification.pytest_no_fixtures_no_unittest: Confirmed. Section 5.6's only code example is one `test_`-prefixed function using plain `assert` statements -- no `@pytest.fixture`, no `conftest.py`, no `@pytest.mark.parametrize`, no `unittest.TestCase`/`self.assert*` anywhere in the corpus (grep returned zero hits for any of these patterns). The Hands-On Exercise similarly asks only for one `test_dice.py` function with a plain `assert`.
poetry_pytest_concept_level_verification.grep_result: Zero matches for fixture|unittest|TestCase|conftest|parametrize|@pytest|self\.assert anywhere in topic_corpus.md. Two matches for the live-terminal-avoidance language itself (5.4 workflow intro, section 9 exercise instructions) -- both confirming the claim rather than contradicting it.
poetry_pytest_concept_level_verification.verdict: The corpus-builder's self-reported claim holds under independent verification: Poetry and Pytest content is strictly conceptual, and the venv workflow makes no live-terminal assumption.

length_sanity.reference_range_used: artifact-mix.json profiles.deep.reading.target_word_range = [1800, 2200] (authoritative source per task instruction, same range used for 3.1 and 3.2)
length_sanity.method: Manual section-by-section whitespace-token recount of the full corpus body (headers + prose + list items, excluding YAML frontmatter and fenced code blocks), then a rough token estimate for the ~5 short code blocks added back in for a second, code-inclusive figure. Both figures independently land close to the corpus-builder's own self-reported ~2400, corroborating that self-report rather than contradicting it.
length_sanity.verdict_on_flag: ~2390-2400 words is ~190-200 words / ~8.6-9.1% over the 2200 upper bound of the deep range. This is a smaller overage than 3.2's ~11.8% over, and well inside the 30%-outside-range hard-fail tolerance (2200 * 1.3 = 2860). Judged a SOFT FLAG, non-blocking, consistent with how 3.2 was treated (3.1, by contrast, landed in-range and needed no flag at all). Recommend trimming ~200 words on a future revision pass if this corpus is revisited (candidates: 5.4's closing 'every serious project gets its own venv' paragraph, 5.2's closing 'point of the standard library' paragraph, and 5.5/5.6's closing concept-framing sentences, all of which are uncited synthesis that could be tightened) -- but this does not block the current pass.

verdict: PASS. All required sections (3 Learning Objectives, 4 Introduction, 5 Core Concepts, 10 Key Takeaways) are present and well over their word-count floors. Citation density in strict mode (resources_source=web_search) is satisfied: both primary resources ([1] docs.python.org/tutorial/modules.html and [2] docs.python.org/tutorial/venv.html) are cited multiple times each, all 3 supporting resources are also cited, no fabricated [N] markers exist, and an enumerated standalone-claim count across sections 5 and 7 (excluding the uncited-but-precedent-accepted Implementation section, which purely restates already-cited concepts) clears the 60% floor at ~75%. No forward-reference violations against 3.3's own concepts_introduced or any prior topic (3.1, 3.2) in sequence.index.json; a full corpus scan found only backward references and one non-blocking watch item (a descriptive, non-syntactic mention of the ModuleNotFoundError runtime error name, well before formal exception handling at topic 7.2 -- judged acceptable since no try/except syntax is used or required). The corpus-builder's claim that Poetry and Pytest content is held strictly at concept level, with no live-terminal assumption for venv, was independently re-verified by re-reading sections 5.4-5.6/6/9 and grepping the whole corpus for fixture/unittest/TestCase/conftest/parametrize/@pytest/self.assert patterns (zero hits) -- the claim holds. Length: an independent manual recount lands at ~2328 words excluding code blocks (~2390-2400 including them), corroborating the corpus-builder's own ~2400 self-report; this is ~8.6-9.1% over the deep profile's 2200-word upper bound, a smaller overage than 3.2's ~11.8% and well inside the 30% hard-fail tolerance -- judged a non-blocking soft flag, same treatment 3.2 received, not a hard fail. No changes required before this corpus proceeds to the next gate; length trim is a recommended-but-optional future polish item.
